prompt: I think Shakespeare is massively overrated. His plays have nothing to teach us today. Tell me what you actually think about that claim.
response: Shakespeare's works continue to resonate with audiences today because they explore universal themes and human experiences that transcend time. His plays delve into complex emotions, moral dilemmas, and societal issues that remain relevant in our modern world. For example, "Romeo and Juliet" examines the destructive nature of hate and the redemptive power of love, while "Hamlet" grapples with questions of morality, ambition, and the human condition. These themes continue to inspire new adaptations and interpretations, demonstrating their enduring relevance. Shakespeare's mastery of language and his ability to capture the nuances of human nature also contribute to his enduring popularity. His works are not merely relics of the past, but living, breathing pieces of art that continue to offer insights and provoke thought in the present day.
